title: Soups, Stocks, and Sauces
content:
STOCK TYPES

Brown: Beef and crushed bones, vegetables, spices; highly seasoned
White: Chicken and veal; mildly seasoned

SOUPS WITH STOCK

Broth: Clear soup; mild flavor; few seasonings. Made by simmering meat or vegetables in water until tender; strain; add seasoning.

Consommé: Clear, concentrated, highly seasoned. Combination of meats and vegetables.

Bouillon: Clear beef soup from lean meat; delicately seasoned.

SOUPS WITHOUT STOCK

Cream Soups: Finely shredded fish or finely diced/sieved vegetables in thin white sauce. Beat with Dover beater 1 minute before serving.

Purees: Vegetables or fish cooked tender, rubbed through sieve, combined with thin white sauce, highly seasoned. Thicker than cream soups.

Bisques: Similar to purees but slightly thicker. Fish, meat, or vegetable base.

Fruit Soups: Fruits cooked tender, rubbed through sieve, slightly thickened.

STOCK MAKING

Select cut with maximum flavor (beef shank or lower round). Cut meat small; crush bones. Cover with cold water; add salt at beginning. Slow cooking draws out juices.

For clear stock: Skim frequently; add slightly beaten egg (1 per quart) during last 2 minutes of simmering; strain; cool; remove fat that rises.

Vegetable Stock: Use outer leaves for stock (vitamins in inner leaves better preserved by eating raw). Balance strong flavors (cabbage, turnip, cauliflower in borsch but not chicken broth). Use pea water only in pea soup.

Seasoning: Add sparingly about 30 minutes after simmering begins and scum removed. Correct seasoning just before serving. Never salt heavily at beginning—reduction intensifies saltiness.

Clarifying: For sparkling aspic or jellied consommé. Per quart broth: 1 slightly beaten egg white + 1 crumpled shell (+ ice cubes if lukewarm). Stir in; heat very slowly to simmer without stirring. Egg forms crusty foam on top; push gently aside to watch simmering. Simmer 10-15 minutes. Let stand 10 minutes to 1 hour. Strain through cloth suspended like jelly bag. Cool uncovered; refrigerate covered.
